2877.d: Years spent in the possession of baleful monsters have lent an ill-boding glint to this once-pure precious metal.

2878.d: Luxurious and smooth to the touch, this fabric is such a deep red as to make one wonder if it were dyed with blood.

2881.d: A brilliant, flawless gem of otherworldly beauty that was a treasured possession of Verthandi.

2884.d: The massive, still-smoldering shell of the gargantuan Krabkatoa, as solid and durable as a slab of volcanic rock.

2885.d: The imposing mandible of the mammoth antlion Orcus, suited to instantaneously crushing the life out of unwitting prey.

2886.d: The excess mold growth on this shabby-looking ingot makes it impossible to discern what sort of metal it originally was.

2887.d: A decaying ingot of some indeterminate metal that feels as if it is about to crumble to dust in your hand.

2888.d: A translucent mass of tissue found protruding from the body of certain vorageans. Though slightly cracked, it retains a vivid scarlet hue.

2889.d: The spiny pincer of a limule.

2890.d: The delicate, translucent wing of a clionid.

2891.d: The withered husk of a dead chigoe.

2892.d: This bloody slab of ram meat exudes an overpowering raw odor.

2893.d: The oversized fang of a gargantuan species of black tiger.

2894.d: A shield claimed by a Gigas off the body of a particularly renowned adventurer it slew.

2895.d: This oversized sock of a Gigas reeks of blood and sweat. Far too large (and rancid) to be worn except by Gigas.

2896.d: A weighted armband used by Gigas in building their hulking muscles. Far too heavy to be worn except by Gigas.

2897.d: An insect wing so transparent as to be almost invisible to the eye.

2898.d: A pitch-black scale that seems to smother the light around it.

2899.d: Puk eggs laid in winter, such as this one, are said to be particularly rich in nutrients.

2901.d: A particularly large and earthy agaricus mushroom.

2902.d: This massive gnole claw is caked with dirt and grime.

2903.d: Blood from an eft, said to be so vile that even leeches steer clear of it.

2904.d: The long, slimy eyestalk of a giant slug.

2905.d: A gossamer astral fabric that glows only under the light of the moon. Far too delicate to use in crafting.

2906.d: An eyeball from a treant that has begun to decompose.

2908.d: A poisonous fungus said to wipe clean the memory of any who eats it. Not suitable for consumption.

2909.d: A curious mottled moss that had been growing from the back of a goobbue.

2910.d: The massive horn of a wivre, of fearsome size and sharpness.

2911.d: A skin that appears to have been savagely ripped from the body of the eft it once belonged to.

2912.d: The particularly gargantuan tusk of a bugard.

2913.d: A murky lens from the eye of an Ahriman.

2914.d: A small globule rumored to be the soul of a deep sea creature. Place it by your ear, and you can hear the sound of a murmuring stream...

2917.d: The bloodshot eyeball of a hecteyes.

2918.d: The cursed skull of a corpselight.

2919.d: The fang of a bat, flecked with fresh blood.

2920.d: The strong alkaline concentration of this humus renders it highly unsuitable for gardening.

2921.d: Still dripping with potent venom, this scorpion stinger must be handled with care.

2922.d: In spite of (or perhaps because of?) the slight sulphurous odor it exudes, eft eggs such as this one are considered a delicacy.

2923.d: Meat from the tail of a cockatrice. Stringy and flavorless, it is quite unpopular among culinarians.

2924.d: This whisker cut from the face of a coeurl pulsates with energy in your hand.

2925.d: A particularly pliant and durable manticore mane.

2926.d: A fragment of some unidentifiable organic substance that emits a faint moaning sound.

2927.d: The massive shell of a glavoid.

2928.d: An oversized flower from the crown of a chloris.

2929.d: An imposing helm that once guarded the head of the legendary Gigas Briareus. Only wearable by Gigas.

2930.d: A small yet tasteful gem that belonged to the evil pixie queen Carabosse.

2931.d: A thick, foul-smelling liquid expelled from a fistule.

2932.d: The massive fang of Kukulkan, capable of impaling a knight clean through his armor.

2933.d: Laid by Chukwa, this egg is several orders of magnitude larger than a typical adamantoise egg.

2934.d: The oversized necklet of a manananggal. An incantation of warding prevents any but its original owner from equipping it.

2935.d: The ratty, flea-ridden pelage of Mangy-tailed Marvin.

2936.d: A dagger-like stinger harvested from the body of a chasmic hornet.

2937.d: The giant vine of a raskovnik.

2938.d: A wing clipped from the imp lord Bakka.

2939.d: The fearsome horn of the legendary serpent Cuelebre.

2940.d: The dark robes worn by the necromancer Mictlantecuhtli while performing his dark rituals. Not wearable by living beings.

2941.d: A pamama purloined from the possession of the fruit-fancying Keesha Poppo.

2942.d: A hefty silver nugget removed from the stomach of Megamaw Mikey. Likely the cause of a mega-serious case of indigestion.

2943.d: The ghastly skull of a balaur wyvern.

2944.d: Once carried by a Tonberry Lieje, this lantern still burns with an ominous, hateful light.

2945.d: The wing of a clionid, now shriveled from excess exposure to the air.

2946.d: The pincer of a limule, now tarnished from excess exposure to the air.

2947.d: This corpselight skull has been cleansed of the curse that once resided in it.

2948.d: Mildly acidic in composition, this humus is well suited for gardening.

2949.d: This eft egg seems about to hatch at any moment...

2950.d: Drained of electricity, this coeurl whisker is soft and smooth to the touch.

2951.d: This spectacular green-colored mineral shimmers brilliantly even in dim light.

2952.d: A gaseous substance that acts to temporarily disperse miasmas.

2953.d: A slimy substance expectorated by a rare species of arachnid. Can be used as an adhesive.

2954.d: A small casket of gunpowder. Dampened from time spent underwater, it is likely unusable in its current state.

2955.d: An elaborate crest belonging to the uppermost nobility of the Kindred.

2956.d: Surreal, nightmarish patterns adorn this crest, symbolizing a Kindred house of the purest lineage.

2957.d: The demonically frightful patterns etched into this crest skillfully convey the power possessed by the unholiest of Kindred houses.

2961.d: The fearsome spike of a peiste, dripping with both venom and the blood of the creature's prey.

2963.d: A massive fang extracted from the mouth of the giant sandworm Ulhuadshi.

2964.d: The skin of the gargantuan bugard Sobek, as rugged as the face of a mountain.

2965.d: The eerie light stalk of Cirein-croin, employed by this fearsome deep sea hunter to lure prey of all sizes.

2966.d: The massive wing of the gargantuan taurus known as Bukhis.

3076.d: Cracked and chipped bones of various size, shape, and origin.

3077.d: When you rest your ear on these tattered garments, you can hear a tortured scream in the distance…

3078.d: A massive force must have been required to cause this sturdy scale of a dragon to crack.

3079.d: This scorpion pincer is caked with the blood of its prey.

3080.d: The fully extended eyestalk of a slug, doomed never to return to its retracted state.

3081.d: This round cut of thigh meat from a coeurl is laced with fat and gristle.

3082.d: Separated from the mandragora it sprouted from, it looks about to crumble into dust at any moment.

3083.d: The cockatrice this skin was taken from clearly did not go down without a fight…

3084.d: The thick root of an ancient treant.

3085.d: The ensorcelled tusk of a bugard.

3086.d: Scraps from the shedded molt of a lizard.

3087.d: These underfeathers of an apkallu are soft and warm to the touch.

3088.d: A large feather that allows birds to control their flight path in strong wind.

3089.d: The rare cheekmeat of an orobon, considered a delicacy.

3091.d: The frozen, hardened skin of a raptor.

3094.d: A chunk of rock salt of the highest grade and quality.

3095.d: This hole-ridden mandragora bud has clearly served as a feast for many a famished worm.

3102.d: This tentacle has stiffened after the death of the soulflayer it was once attached to.

3109.d: The particularly sturdy hide of a rabbit.

Filled to the brim with jingly in hopes of a prosperous new year.

3930.d: Despite its near-invisible size, this twitherym wing is a beauty to behold.

3934.d: A hollow shell that once served as a suit of armor for a ferocious matamata. It has taken its fair share of beatings, but is none the worse for the wear.

3941.d: This wing torn from the back of a chapuli is as light as a feather and flexible enough to survive tempestuous gales.

3942.d: An interesting specimen of creature that resembles a chapuli. Though normally associated with death and destruction, this type of beast possesses a certain organ which aids in flushing out poisons.

3951.d: Cold to the touch, this adumbral stone emits faint, ghoulish screams that pioneers liken to a convocation of the damned.

3958.d: This mineral-rich soil taken from the Yahse Hunting Grounds is as nutritious as it is natural.

3959.d: Meat sliced efficiently from the body of one of the Zoldeff River's tastiest inhabitants--the Delectable Orobon.

3960.d: This stony hunk of a petrified tree contains flecks of a substance that emits an unsettlingly spectral green glow.

3961.d: This stony hunk of a petrified tree contains flecks of a substance that emits an unsettlingly spectral blue glow.

3962.d: This stony hunk of a petrified tree contains flecks of a substance that emits an unsettlingly spectral red glow.

3965.d: A type of seaweed that is often harvested along the shores of the Ulbukan continent.

3966.d: Glasses used in the process of measuring soul pyres.

Tradeable for spoils from bosses in Delve.

3974.d: This mystical and magical solution may be as intangible as the air itself, but it still draws out immense powers that lay dormant within certain pieces of equipment.

3975.d: This mystical and magical solution may be as intangible as the air itself, but it still draws out immense powers that lay dormant within certain pieces of equipment.

3976.d: This mystical and magical solution may be as intangible as the air itself, but it still draws out immense powers that lay dormant within certain pieces of equipment.

3977.d: The mafic horn of a culled gabbrath that remains doused in the blood of the countless pioneers unfortunate enough to be impaled upon it.

3978.d: A rigid dorsal fin with a surprisingly clean and soft shape. It once belonged to a rockfin before the beast was fished up from the depths of the unknown.

3979.d: Sharper than the most finely whetted blade, this rockfin tooth can slice through an unwary pioneer's fingers in an instant.

3980.d: Once attached to the abdomen of an enraged bztavian, this stinger would make even the winds retreat in fear when thrust.

3981.d: This wing ripped forcefully from the back of a bztavian would cause roaring gales to erupt whenever flapped.

3982.d: That this seed will produce vegetation is known. Where the seed came from, however, is not.

3983.d: That this seed will spring forth fruit is known. Where the seed came from, however, is not.

3984.d: A pouch containing the seeds of a golden yellow flower.

3985.d: Viscous, noxious drool scraped from the mouth of a massive insect that slobbers uncontrollably. Slathering it on mineral veins attracts antlions.

3986.d: A fragrant fluid that releases an odor many insects crave. Placing it in an arboreal grove makes it possible to harvest beetles occasionally.

3988.d:
A long and lithe slimy slitherer that enriches the soil wherever it tunnels.
Increases the yield of crops from a furrow.

3989.d:
This humus apparently has no official origin, as scholars have yet to ascertain its native habitat.
Reduces the length of a furrow's grow season.

3990.d: This spectacular example of humus was released upon Ulbuka by the Inventors' Coalition after months of careful research, which have paid off in spades. Occasionally produces strange and wondrous harvests.

3991.d:
A long and lithe slimy slitherer that enriches the soil wherever it tunnels.
Increases the bounty obtained from a grove.

3992.d:
This specialized mulch works even in the unforgiving Ulbukan soil, keeping the trees growing straight and strong.
Occasionally increases the number of times one can log a grove.

3993.d:
Cuttings from trees nestled in an unspecified forest somewhere in Vana'diel.
Enables lumber to be more easily procured from groves.

3994.d:
This thick and viscous nectar emits an aroma so enticing that many pioneers begin to slaver simply at the thought of tasting it.
Allows insects to be more easily procured from groves.

3995.d:
This spectacular example of fertilizer was released upon Ulbuka by the Inventors' Coalition after months of careful research, which have paid off in spades.
Occasionally allows for strange and wondrous finds to flourish in a grove.

3996.d:
A solution that magically softens the surface of mineral veins and primes them for mining.
Increases the yield of a vein when mined.

3997.d:
A solution that magically stiffens the surface of mineral veins, which keeps them from degrading quickly.
Occasionally increases the number of times a vein can be mined.

3998.d:
A solution that transmogrifies the surface of mineral veins, completely changing their composition.
Allows for precious metals to be more easily procured while mining.

3999.d:
A solution that transmogrifies the surface of mineral veins, completely changing their composition.
Allows for metals to be more easily procured while mining.

4000.d:
This spectacular example of a serum was released upon Ulbuka by the Inventors' Coalition after months of careful research, which have paid off in spades.
Occasionally allows for strange and wondrous finds to flourish when mining.

4001.d: A ball of ground sardine mixed with grains specifically added to attract fish inhabiting freshwater lakes and ponds.

4002.d: A ball of ground bass mixed with grains specifically added to attract fish inhabiting freshwater lakes and ponds.

4003.d: A ball of ground shrimp mixed with grains specifically added to attract fish inhabiting freshwater lakes and ponds.

4004.d: A ball of ground mincemeat mixed with grains specifically added to attract fish inhabiting freshwater lakes and ponds.

4005.d:
This spectacular example of rolled bait was released upon Ulbuka by the Inventors' Coalition after months of careful research, which have paid off in spades.
Occasionally allows for strange and wondrous catches when fishing in freshwater.

4006.d: A sack of mangled sardine mixed with grains specifically added to attract saltwater fish.

4007.d: A sack of mangled bass mixed with grains specifically added to attract saltwater fish.

4008.d: A sack of mangled shrimp mixed with grains specifically added to attract saltwater fish.

4009.d: A sack of mincemeat slapped together and mixed with grains specifically added to attract saltwater fish.

4010.d:
This spectacular example of chum was released upon Ulbuka by the Inventors' Coalition after months of careful research, which have paid off in spades.
Occasionally allows for strange and wondrous catches when fishing in saltwater.

4011.d:
This spectacular example of a catalytic enzyme was released upon Ulbuka by the Inventors' Coalition after months of careful research, which have paid off in spades.
Makes crops harvestable in almost no time.

4012.d: Though this beak was already torn violently from the head of a conquered waktza, lightning still crackles and darts around it, temporarily paralyzing any who dare to touch its dagger-blade surface.

4013.d: Once crowning the head of a proud waktza, this delicately soft feather belies the ferocity of its former master. Due to its difficulty to obtain, many fortunate enough to get their hands upon one employ it as a good luck charm.

4014.d: This sturdy trunk hacked free of a yggdreant after a flurry of agonizing attempts puts the fear of the forest into each and every pioneer who dares steal even a glimpse at it.

4015.d: This knotted and gnarled root, chopped from the base of a fallen yggdreant, is rumored to bestow everlasting life upon those who boil it in their tea.

4017.d: The verdant, two-leafed shoot that emerges from the heads of all mandragoras. Contemporary researchers proffer that its leaves capture sunlight and convert it into a sort of nourishment.

4018.d: A rough-cut log of guatambu timber.

4020.d: This ore contains the metal titanium.

4023.d: This ore contains the precious metal snowsteel.

4026.d:
A grass common throughout the Ulbukan continent.
Fiber taken from its stem is used as
a material for akaso thread.

4028.d: Cloth woven from akaso fiber thread.

4029.d: This jet-black cloth contains the faintest remains of runic sorcery woven into its fabric.

4030.d: Originating from a country in the Far East, this blood red and pitch black coating, made from the sap of the lacquer tree, is often used on wooden crafts.

4031.d: The digestive juices that rest inside a snapweed's mouth and trap whatever prey is unfortunate enough to wander inside.

4032.d: The still-twitching and vine-like limb of a snapweed.

4033.d: Cold to the touch, this sickly green stone is as ghoulishly powerful as it is frigid.

4034.d: Cold to the touch, this sickly green stone is as ghoulishly powerful as it is frigid.

4035.d: Cold to the touch, this sickly green stone is as ghoulishly powerful as it is frigid.

4036.d: The wing of a black twitherym that flutters within the deepest recesses of otherworldly skirmishes. Its inconceivably dark color often reminds pioneers how pathetically miniscule they are in the grand scheme of things.

4037.d: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

4038.d: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

4039.d: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

4040.d: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

4041.d: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

4042.d: This ore developed incalculable veins of enigmatic sorcerous minerals while gestating in the stomach of an acuex.

4043.d: A resplendent jewel streaked with slowly snaking lines of magma.

4044.d: Its inky slick appearance and metallic lustre are but two clues to the subterranean powers this rock harbors, such as mitigating the forces of water.

4045.d: Forcefully shorn from its stem by tempestuous winds that churn with the power of the heavens, this bulb of cotton came from locales unknown.

4046.d: Dyed a bleeding red hue from various chemical reactions, this craggy and porous mass of minerals has an appearance so miserable that not even a miner could commiserate with it.

4047.d: An extremely strong and incredibly durable thread of silk harvested from erucas that slink about the crevasses of Yorcia Weald.

4048.d: Speckled with bumps around its eyes, this fish can be found in the rivers and lakes of the Ulbukan continent's archipelago. Many marine life maniacs, including the Green Thumb Moogle, covet this precious fish.

4049.d: An ancient piece of currency from the east, hauled in from the depths of the ocean and coated with a heavy layer of rust. Coin collectors like the Green Thumb Moogle may find this of interest.

4050.d: So rotund that it would hardly fit in a Meeble's mouth, this rare specimen of burrow worm is prized by not only the burrow diggers themselves, but by the Green Thumb Moogle as well.

4051.d: This mystical solution is inexplicably absorbed by myriad metals, which seems to be how it draws out the immense powers that lay dormant within certain pieces of equipment.

4052.d: This mystical solution is inexplicably absorbed by myriad metals, which seems to be how it draws out the immense powers that lay dormant within certain pieces of equipment.

4053.d: This mystical solution is inexplicably absorbed by myriad metals, which seems to be how it draws out the immense powers that lay dormant within certain pieces of equipment.

4054.d: The crystallized soul of a beetle as black as midnight. It squirms so incessantly within its mineral prison that it emits a palpable warmth.

4055.d: The crystallized soul of an antlion as black as midnight. It squirms so incessantly within its mineral prison that it emits a palpable warmth.

4056.d: The crystallized soul of a pachypodium as black as midnight. It squirms so incessantly within its mineral prison that it emits a palpable warmth.

4057.d: A frigid crystalline mass, stones of this type occasionally contain pellucid gems that create dazzling displays of light.

4058.d: Dizzying numbers of veins containing the mineral bismuth crisscross the interior of this ore.

4059.d: This naturally annealed conglomeration was formed from magma that once coursed through deep subterranean channels.

4060.d: This somber hunk of ore contains an extremely rare type of iron that rumors say will revolutionize the smithing process.

4061.d: A shiny, jet-black gemstone wrested from the abyssal depths of an unexplored crevasse.

4062.d: A gem so solid that dropping it from a fair height supposedly leaves fissures in the immediate area.

4063.d: A gem so solid that dropping it from a fair height supposedly leaves fissures in the earth for malms.

4064.d: The first chapter of ten in the saga of an ordinary man named Rem. The kingdom is fraught with chaos, as a long-ruling king collapses. His death engenders a terrible power struggle, from which Rem miraculously emerges the victor.

4065.d: The second chapter of ten in the saga of an ordinary man named Rem. In it, the leader of a neighboring empire takes advantage of Rem's inexperience and invades his homeland.

4066.d: The third chapter of ten in the saga of an ordinary man named Rem. In it, Rem's kingdom is unceremoniously routed by the invaders and annexed to their territory.

4067.d: The fourth chapter of ten in the saga of an ordinary man named Rem. In it, Rem is stripped of his crown and imprisoned within his own palace's oubliette.

4068.d: The fifth chapter of ten in the saga of an ordinary man named Rem. In it, the protagonist struggles to maintain his sanity while locked in what is ostensibly a granite-lined hole for a full decade.

4069.d: The sixth chapter of ten in the saga of an ordinary man named Rem. In it, a rowdy band of vagabonds who long for the good old days of Rem's rule slips into the oubliette under the cover of darkness and saves him in his eleventh year of imprisonment.

4070.d: The seventh chapter of ten in the saga of an ordinary man named Rem. In it, news of Rem's rescue rings throughout the land and galvanizes the masses to stand up in resistance to the neighboring tyrant.

4071.d: The eighth chapter of ten in the saga of an ordinary man named Rem. In it, the hefty number of resistance fighters rise up against their oppressive foreign king.

4072.d: The ninth chapter of ten in the saga of an ordinary man named Rem. In it, the resistance movement succeeds against all odds. The well-trained phalanxes of infantry from the neighboring kingdom lie battered and defeated outside the castle gates.

4073.d: The final chapter of ten in the saga of an ordinary man named Rem. In it, the crown is returned to Rem thirteen years after the whole ordeal began. Everyone lives happily ever after under his fair and just rule.

5120.d:
Feared by sailors and pirates alike,
this giant sea monster has been known
to tear apart ships with its razor-sharp
teeth.

This tropical fish makes its home
among the coral of the Maliyakaleya
Reef. The Tarutaru love to keep them
as pets.

This echinoderm is found all over

not find any gracing the menu

sands of Bibiki Bay. It scares away
predators by shooting out of its
shell like a rocket.

gathering has led to a decline in their
numbers.

5949.d: A type of bivalve, this shellfish can be found clinging desperately to boulders and reefs across the islands that dot Ulbuka's coastline.

5950.d: This saltwater fish makes its home in the Senroh Sea, which may just be the reason its smoked and sliced brethren taste so delicious.

5951.d: This gigantic fish that swims off the shores off Foret de Hennetiel intimidates not only with its size, but also the vast amounts of deadly poison it stores within its body.

5952.d: A poisonous freshwater fish that populates the Zoldeff River in Foret de Hennetiel.

5953.d: A freshwater fish whose fins resemble the delicate wings of a dragonfly.

5954.d: An arthropod that once clung desperately to an underwater prominence in the Senroh Sea.

5955.d: Roaming the waters deep within Ulbuka's continental rifts, this freshwater fish is surprisingly elusive for its massive size.

5957.d: A long, thin fish whose scales can emit light electric pulses.

5959.d: Found mainly in estuaries, this fish is adorned with scales so refulgent that fishkeepers all over Adoulin covet its brethren.

5960.d: A crustacean that thrives on the Ulbukan continent, possibly due to the strength of the poison it possesses, which can incapacitate creatures much larger than itself.

5961.d: A miniscule cephalopod that roams the Senroh Sea, protecting its life by squeezing into the tiniest of cracks.

5962.d: This tentacle was ripped mercilessly from a frightened contortopus.

5966.d: This fruit is indigenous to the Ulbukan hinterland, and any who exert the tremendous effort required to bypass its rock-like exterior are rewarded with pulp from a succulent center.

5967.d: Lore has it that those who eat this odd mushroom will inevitably fail to stay their tongues, no matter how hard they try.

6137.d: This leather quiver is used to carry large quantities of chapuli arrows.

6138.d: This leather quiver is used to carry large quantities of mantid arrows.

6139.d: This leather quiver is used to carry large quantities of midrium bolts.

6140.d: This leather quiver is used to carry large quantities of damascus bolts.

6141.d: This leather quiver is used to carry large quantities of oxidant bolts.

6142.d: This leather pouch is used to carry large quantities of midrium bullets.

6143.d: This leather pouch is used to carry large quantities of damascus bullets.

6144.d: The colder the water, the more this fish thrives, for it can only be found in rivers and lakes in Ulbuka that have been frozen over.

6145.d: A new hatchling from the remora, a species of fish that has managed to survive for countless eons.

6146.d: A legendary fish that has bloodied the waters in which it swims since times of yore.

6147.d: A memo scrawled by Mikhe Aryohcha that matter-of-factly states, "Just throw your fist at your opponent and it'll all work out." Adventurers say that their hand-to-hand skill increases after reading this note.

6148.d: A guide to the finer points of wielding a dagger, written by an anonymous individual. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's dagger skill.

6149.d: Ulla wrote this guide on sword wielding--from how to grip the hilt to tips on footwork--so others could follow in her footsteps. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's sword skill.

6150.d: An account penned by Mieuseloir B Enchelles of his encounters with the Gigas in Beaucedine Glacier. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's great sword skill.

6151.d: An account penned by Striking Bull during the Second Battle of Konschtat. It details the Republic's victory over King Raigegue the Lupine's San d'Orian army. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's axe skill.

6152.d: A guide to the finer points of hacking everything in one's way to pieces with a two-handed axe, written by an anonymous individual. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's great axe skill.

6153.d: A report made by Ludwig Eichberg regarding the Battle of Grauberg. It details the constant changes in the position of the front line and the withdrawal of Republic troops. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's scythe skill.

6154.d: An article detailing the duel between  Yrvaulair S Cousseraux and Alphonimile M Aurchiat that became all the rage in San d'Oria after appearing in a popular newspaper. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's polearm skill.

6155.d: A diary written by Kagetora. In it he details each and every one of his ninety-eight victories and ninety-nine losses against Yomi. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's katana skill.

6156.d: A record Noillurie kept of her personal experiences. The one that stands out the most is the battle with the colossal beast she undertook to prove herself to her order. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's great katana skill.

6157.d: A diary written by Ferreous Coffin that describes his encounters with Orcs in the north. So many were there that his war hammer became coated with a thick layer of blood after all was said and done. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's club skill.

6158.d: Memoirs penned by Kayeel-Payeel. They describe in particular detail the time he received Claustrum from the Warlock Warlord Robel-Akbel. Adventurers note that reading them increases one's staff skill.

6159.d: A guidebook Perih Vashai jotted down for the edification of new recruits. It discusses everything from the various ways of holding a bow to methods of judging distance. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's archery skill.

6160.d: An educational text authored by Elivira Gogol. It discusses how to dismantle, clean, and reconstruct firearms in careful detail. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's marksmanship skill.

6161.d: A guide to the finer points of hurling projectile objects at opponents, written by an anonymous individual. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's throwing skill.

6162.d: A memo scrawled by Mikhe Aryohcha that matter-of-factly states, "Just throw your guard up and it'll all work out." Adventurers note that reading it increases one's guarding skill.

6163.d: A compilation of memories from Sonia. Of particular note are her observations on how to replicate the sheer brilliance of Annika Brilioth and the esoteric steps known as the Kriegstanz. Adventurers say that reading it increases their evasion skill.

6164.d: An essay authored by Cerane I Virgaut, mainly concerning the night Perseus bequeathed unto her an exemplary shield. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's shield skill.

6165.d: A journal kept by Kagetora that delineates the extent to which he and Yomi lost themselves in their studies of the martial arts. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's parrying skill.

6166.d: A copy of Febrenard C Brunnaut's favorite read. The simple lyrics and enchanting meter were said to have soothed his soul. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's divine magic skill.

6167.d: A collection of thoughts scribbled out by Ferreous Coffin on his visit to Coveffe Barrows. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's healing magic skill.

6168.d: A guide to the finer points of heightening the potential of one's compatriots, written by Rainemard. It also includes some tips on how to swing special swords. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's enhancing magic skill.

6169.d: A collection of observations made by Rainemard on a particular exploration. It goes into such detail on so much minutiae that many admit to never finishing it. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's enfeebling magic skill.

6170.d: A simple list of known criminals who are better off dead. Every single name is crossed out in Azima's handwriting. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's elemental magic skill.

6171.d: A guide to the finer points of insidious dark magic, as compiled by Azima. Proceeds from this tome have gone to fund her various purchases dealing with alchemical research. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's dark magic skill.

6172.d: A musical score composed by Lewenhart. Its notes symbolize a fragrant, early morning summer breeze. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's singing skill.

6173.d: A musical score composed by Lewenhart. Its notes symbolize the damp and musty air that stagnates within an underground cave. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's string instrument skill.

6174.d: A musical score composed by Lewenhart. Its notes symbolize the gently glowing beams of light that filter through the leaves of a deciduous tree in the late afternoon. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's wind instrument skill.

6175.d: A meticulously drawn diagram Yomi made for Kagetora explaining how to construct certain ninja tools. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's ninjutsu skill.

6176.d: A specious work written by an unknown individual that both reads and feels like an illusion from the ancient past. It discusses the world avatars inhabit, but seems too absurd to be true. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's summoning magic skill.

6177.d: A report written and filed by an anonymous individual. It covers the behavioral patterns and diets of almost every known family of beasts in existence. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's blue magic skill.

6178.d: A record of what happened on the fateful day the lifestream overflowed, as kept by Hrohj Wagrehsa. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's geomancy skill.
